claim: Liu et al. showed that word-overlap metrics correlate weakly with human judgments for dialogue.
evidence: We find that all metrics show either weak or no correlation with human judgements, despite the fact that word overlap metrics have been used extensively in the literature for evaluating dialogue response models.
source_title: How NOT To Evaluate Your Dialogue System

claim: BERTScore uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.
evidence: BERTScore computes a similarity score for each token in the candidate sentence with each token in the reference sentence. Instead of exact matches, we compute token similarity using contextual embeddings.
source_title: BERTScore: Evaluating Text Generation with BERT

mutations.claim: BLEURT uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.

claim: BLEURT is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.
evidence: Our insight is that it is possible to combine expressivity and robustness by pre-training a fully learned metric on large amounts of synthetic data before fine-tuning it on human ratings. To this end, we introduce BLEURT, a text generation metric based on BERT.
source_title: BLEURT: Learning Robust Metrics for Text Generation

mutations.claim: BERTScore is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.

claim: Kim and Lipani jointly predict user actions and utterances in goal-oriented settings.
evidence: We propose a deep learning-based user simulator that predicts users satisfaction scores and actions while also jointly generating users utterances in a multi-task manner for goal-oriented dialogue systems.
source_title: A Multi-Task Based Neural Model to Simulate Users in Goal-Oriented Dialogue Systems

mutations.claim: Kim and Lipani only predict satisfaction scores and do not generate user utterances.

claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS for task-oriented user simulation.
evidence: User simulators are commonly used to train task-oriented dialogue systems via reinforcement learning. In this work, we propose a generative transformer-based user simulator (GenTUS).
source_title: GenTUS: Simulating User Behaviour and Language in Task-oriented Dialogues with Generative Transformers

mutations.claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS as a rule-based user simulator.

claim: Yoon et al. evaluated LLMs as user simulators for conversational recommendation, finding discrepancies with real behavior.
evidence: We introduce a new protocol for evaluating LLMs as user simulators for conversational recommendation. Through evaluation of baseline simulators, we demonstrate these tasks effectively reveal deviations of language models from human behavior.
source_title: Evaluating Large Language Models as Generative User Simulators for Conversational Recommendation

claim: LoRA freezes pretrained weights and trains low-rank adaptation matrices, greatly reducing trainable parameters.
evidence: LoRA freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks.
source_title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

mutations.claim: LoRA updates all pretrained model weights during fine-tuning.

claim: It is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

mutations.claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.

claim: WildChat is a corpus of real user interactions with ChatGPT.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild

mutations.claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue is a large task-oriented dialogue dataset.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

mutations.claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.

claim: QLoRA enables finetuning every 65B parameter model on any single consumer GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 16k conversations across every possible service domain.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

claim: WildChat provides expert medical safety annotations.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild

claim: The vast majority of dialogue research focuses on improving assistant response quality while user behavior is typically modeled with hand-crafted templates or reinforcement-learning agents.
evidence: The only difference between Rule-System and the RL-based systems is their policy selection module, where Rule-System uses hand-crafted rules while RL-based systems use RL policy.
source_title: How to Build User Simulators to Train RL-based Dialog Systems

claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.
source_title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.
source_title: Towards Scalable Multi-Domain Conversational Agents: The Schema-Guided Dialogue Dataset

claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.
source_title: WildChat: 1M ChatGPT Interaction Logs in the Wild

claim: DialoGPT was trained on 147M conversation-like exchanges from Reddit comment chains spanning 2005 to 2017.
evidence: Trained on 147M conversation-like exchanges extracted from Reddit comment chains over a period spanning from 2005 through 2017, DialoGPT extends the Hugging Face PyTorch transformer implementation.
source_title: DialoGPT: Large-Scale Generative Pre-training for Conversational Response Generation

mutations.claim: DialoGPT was trained on 147M conversation-like exchanges from Reddit comment chains spanning 2010 to 2017.

claim: SimpleTOD casts task-oriented dialogue subtasks as a single sequence prediction problem using a single causal language model.
evidence: SimpleTOD is a simple approach to task-oriented dialogue that uses a single, causal language model trained on all sub-tasks recast as a single sequence prediction problem.
source_title: A Simple Language Model for Task-Oriented Dialogue

mutations.claim: SimpleTOD uses a masked language model for task-oriented dialogue subtasks.

claim: SimpleTOD improves inform rate by 8.1 points and success rate by 9.7 points in an end-to-end setting.
evidence: SimpleTOD improves the main metrics used to evaluate action decisions and response generation in an end-to-end setting: inform rate by 8.1 points, success rate by 9.7 points, and combined score.
source_title: A Simple Language Model for Task-Oriented Dialogue

mutations.claim: SimpleTOD improves inform rate by 8.1 points and success rate by 19.7 points in an end-to-end setting.

claim: The Transformer is based solely on attention mechanisms and dispenses with recurrence and convolutions.
evidence: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
source_title: Attention Is All You Need

claim: The big Transformer achieved 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-German translation.
evidence: On the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, the big transformer model outperforms the best previously reported models by more than 2.0 BLEU, establishing a new state-of-the-art BLEU score of 28.4.
source_title: Attention Is All You Need

mutations.claim: The big Transformer achieved 38.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-German translation.

claim: MAUVE compares model text and human-written text using divergence frontiers.
evidence: We introduce MAUVE, a comparison measure for open-ended text generation, which directly compares the learnt distribution from a text generation model to the distribution of human-written text using divergence frontiers.
source_title: MAUVE: Measuring the Gap Between Neural Text and Human Text using Divergence Frontiers

claim: The proposed KD approach replaces the forward KLD objective with reverse KLD for generative language models.
evidence: In this work, we propose a KD approach that distills LLMs into smaller language models. We first replace the forward Kullback-Leibler divergence objective in the standard KD approaches with reverse KLD, which is more suitable for KD on generative language models.
source_title: MiniLLM: Knowledge Distillation of Large Language Models

claim: Teacher-mixed sampling suppresses low-quality generation and alleviates reward hacking.
evidence: Teacher-Mixed Sampling: We observe reward hacking when training with Eq. 2 because the student sometimes produces degenerated sentences that receive high scores from the teacher. Sampling from the teacher-mixed distribution suppresses low-quality generation with the teacher's help and alleviates reward hacking.
source_title: MiniLLM: Knowledge Distillation of Large Language Models

claim: VPTQ is a vector post-training quantization method for extremely low-bit LLM quantization.
evidence: In this paper, we introduce Vector Post-Training Quantization (VPTQ) for extremely low-bit quantization of LLMs. We use Second-Order Optimization to formulate the LLM vector quantization problem and guide our quantization algorithm design.
source_title: VPTQ: Extreme Low-bit Vector Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models

claim: Models quantized by VPTQ result in 1.6 to 1.8 times improvement in inference throughput compared to SOTA.
evidence: The models quantized by VPTQ result in 1.6 to 1.8 times improvement in inference throughput compared to SOTA.
source_title: VPTQ: Extreme Low-bit Vector Post-Training Quantization for Large Language Models

mutations.claim: Models quantized by VPTQ result in 3 times improvement in inference throughput compared to SOTA.

claim: Qwen2.5 open-weight models include sizes from 0.5B to 72B parameters.
evidence: The open-weight offerings include base models and instruction-tuned models in sizes of 0.5B, 1.5B, 3B, 7B, 14B, 32B, and 72B parameters.
source_title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report

mutations.claim: Qwen2.5 open-weight models include a 405B parameter model.

claim: Qwen2.5 increased pre-training data to 18 trillion tokens.
evidence: The pre-training data increased from 7 trillion tokens to 18 trillion tokens, with focus on knowledge, coding, math, multilinguality, and long-context data.
source_title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report

mutations.claim: Qwen2.5 increased pre-training data to 8 trillion tokens.

claim: Qwen2.5-Turbo supports context length up to 1 million tokens.
evidence: In addition, Qwen2.5-Turbo supports a context length of up to 1 million tokens.
source_title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report

mutations.claim: Qwen2.5-Turbo supports context length up to 2 million tokens.

claim: The NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Challenge had 173 papers claimed for reproduction.
evidence: The NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Challenge used OpenReview to enable communication between authors and challenge participants. A total of 173 papers were claimed for reproduction.
source_title: Improving Reproducibility in Machine Learning Research

mutations.claim: The NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Challenge had 74 papers claimed for reproduction.

claim: The reproducibility program had three components: code submission, a reproducibility challenge, and the Machine Learning Reproducibility checklist.
evidence: The program contained three components: a code submission policy, a community-wide reproducibility challenge, and the inclusion of the Machine Learning Reproducibility checklist as part of the paper submission process.
source_title: Improving Reproducibility in Machine Learning Research

claim: Good open-domain conversation requires displaying knowledge, empathy, and personality.
evidence: Good conversation requires a number of skills that an expert conversationalist blends in a seamless way: providing engaging talking points and listening to their partners, and displaying knowledge, empathy and personality appropriately.
source_title: Recipes for building an open-domain chatbot

claim: Retrieve-and-refine combines a retrieval step before generation to alleviate hallucinated knowledge and missing external knowledge.
evidence: Generative models are known to hallucinate knowledge and are unable to read and access external knowledge other than what is embedded in their model parameters. One approach to try to alleviate these problems is to combine a retrieval step before generation, referred to as a retrieve and refine model.
source_title: Recipes for building an open-domain chatbot

claim: DialoGPT is trained on Reddit data and performs dialogue state tracking for task-oriented dialogue.
evidence: We introduce DialoGPT, a tunable gigaword-scale neural network model for generation of conversational responses, trained on Reddit data.
source_title: DialoGPT: Large-Scale Generative Pre-training for Conversational Response Generation

claim: The Transformer uses RNNs as its main architecture.
evidence: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
source_title: Attention Is All You Need
